name: Amp
description: Sourcegraph's frontier coding agent; start an agent in the terminal and control it from web, CLI, and mobile.

developer.comment: Built by Sourcegraph; spun off into its own company (Amp Frontier Corp) on Dec 2, 2025. Checked 2026-06-05.

first-release.comment: Research preview launched May 2025; Amp Free (free for everyone) followed in Oct 2025.

status.comment: Actively developed; CLI redesigned ('Neo by Amp') in May 2026.

license.comment: Closed-source product; usage-based credits with a free tier (Amp Free).

specialization.comment: Positioned as a general-purpose frontier coding agent.

interface.comment: Terminal-first CLI, IDE extensions (VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, Zed), and a web surface for remote control. Mobile remote control also offered.

host-ides.comment: CLI in terminal; editor extensions for VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, Zed; web/browser for remote control. Also works inside Cursor/Windsurf forks.

open-source.comment: Proprietary; agent source is not openly licensed.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: CLI tool plus IDE extensions; backend runs as a cloud service (ampcode.com on GCP).

default-model-provider.comment: Routes across providers (Claude Opus 4.8 for smart mode, GPT-5.5 for the oracle, plus fast models) rather than a single default.

model-choice.comment: Offers Deep/Smart/Rush modes mapping to different models; user picks the mode rather than arbitrary models.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Primarily billed via Amp credits (zero markup); security page mentions limited own-key use but pricing model is credit-based, not general BYOK. Checked 2026-06-05.

local-model-support.comment: No documented local/self-hosted model support; relies on hosted frontier models.

multi-file-editing.comment: Edits multiple files as part of agentic tasks.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Chains edit-run-fix steps; by default does not ask for approval before running tools.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell commands, builds, and tests as part of its loop.

mcp-support.comment: Supports local and remote MCP servers (command/args or HTTP), bundlable via mcp.json in skills.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Spawns subagents with isolated context for parallel/multi-step work.

codebase-indexing.comment: No explicit semantic-index/embeddings layer documented; uses file mention/search and reads files on demand rather than a built index.

planning-mode.comment: Supports a read-only planning approach (e.g. instructing it not to edit files); not a hard-gated dedicated mode.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts image attachments via clipboard (Ctrl+V) or file mention.

browser-computer-use.comment: Can screenshot localhost URLs and inspect/modify UI from visual feedback.

operating-mode.comment: Interactive CLI/chat plus autonomous execution; Amp Tab provides inline completion; web/mobile enable async remote control of running agents.

inline-completion.comment: Amp Tab provides in-editor tab completion in VS Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

background-async-execution.comment: Agents can be driven/monitored remotely from web and mobile while running.

persistent-memory.comment: Threads persist across sessions with full history; referenceable via URL or @-mention.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: IDE extensions support file editing with undo; thread-based state. Note: relies on editor undo rather than a dedicated whole-run snapshot. Checked 2026-06-05.

git-integration.comment: Git blame, commit history, diff analysis, and automatic commit trailers (configurable).

pr-creation.comment: No explicit documented native PR creation found in the docs reviewed; it can run git operations but PR-opening was not confirmed. Checked 2026-06-05.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md guidance files with @-mentions, glob patterns, and per-file YAML frontmatter.

hooks-extensibility.comment: TypeScript plugins in .amp/plugins/ provide event handling, custom tools, commands, and custom agent modes/subagents.

permission-model.comment: By default does not ask for approval before running tools; plugins can intercept tool.call events and legacy permission rules can be configured. Workspace MCP servers require explicit approval.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Cloud-only at ampcode.com on GCP; no self-hosting/on-prem deployment.

privacy-mode.comment: Enterprise plans guarantee zero data retention / no training on inputs/outputs; non-enterprise users can disable training in settings.

enterprise-controls.comment: SSO (Okta), SCIM directory sync, audit logging, and role-based access controls. No self-hosted VPC (cloud-only).

pricing-model.comment: Usage-based credits (zero markup for individuals/teams) plus a free tier (Amp Free). $5 minimum credit purchase; enterprise +50%.

free-tier.comment: Amp Free launched Oct 2025, free for everyone.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Pure usage-based with no fixed monthly plan ($5 minimum credit purchase, pay-as-you-go); no monthly subscription tier to quote.

maturity.comment: Usable and actively shipping (major CLI redesign May 2026) but still young (first preview May 2025) and rapidly evolving. Checked 2026-06-05.

community-size.comment: Notable Sourcegraph-backed adoption and active community, but smaller than Cursor/Copilot scale. Rough estimate, checked 2026-06-05.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: TypeScript plugin system plus MCP server support enable a third-party ecosystem.

memory-scopes.comment: AGENTS.md supports project-level and per-file/folder granular guidance via glob patterns and YAML frontmatter.

scoped-rules-files.comment: AGENTS.md is always-on; per-file/glob frontmatter enables path-based scoped guidance.

auto-memory.comment: No documented autonomous cross-session memory writing; persistence is via threads and manual AGENTS.md. Checked 2026-06-05.

skill-support.comment: Skills are task-specific agent instruction packages in .agents/skills/ with bundled resources and optional MCP servers; also custom plugin commands.

skill-invocation.comment: Skills/commands can be invoked explicitly and loaded by the agent on demand when relevant.

always-on-surface.comment: Skills are loaded on demand (progressive disclosure) rather than keeping full bodies in context. Inferred from skill package design; checked 2026-06-05.

skill-distribution.comment: Skills live as files in .agents/skills/ (manual/git-distributed); no curated marketplace documented.
